search.placeholder: Search projects by name, tech stack, or objective...

contentSection.heading: How I Work
contentSection.paragraph1: Every project I build starts with a simple premise: understand the problem before writing a single line of code. I work with feature-based architecture, applying DDD-lite principles so the business domain is reflected in the project structure. This means folders and modules speak the same language as the product, not the framework.
contentSection.paragraph2: My tech stack is pragmatic, not hype-driven. I choose TypeScript on both frontend and backend for type safety, React for declarative and predictable interfaces, Node.js with Express for REST APIs, and PostgreSQL as my relational database. I don't follow trends — I pick tools that solve the actual problem with the least overhead possible.
contentSection.paragraph3: Every project goes through research, architecture design, implementation with tests, and automated deployment. I prioritize readable, testable code over premature optimization. My goal is that any developer can read the code and understand not just what it does, but why it's structured that way.

echolog.name: EchoLog
echolog.short: Multi-tenant customer feedback SaaS - boards, upvoting, comments, workspace management. Full-stack TypeScript with React 19, Node.js, Express, PostgreSQL.

nexus-talent.name: Nexus Talent
nexus-talent.short: AI-assisted recruitment platform that turns raw job descriptions into structured skill matrices and technical application strategies.

movie-dashboard.name: Movie Management Dashboard
movie-dashboard.short: SPA dashboard to manage movie catalog. CRUD, Supabase authentication, React Query (cache, fetching), forms and tests.

cinelab.name: CineLab
cinelab.short: Movie Search App - search, filters, persistent favorites and dynamic recommendations (TMDB).

chefcitoia.name: ChefcitoIA
chefcitoia.short: AI recipe generator that turns ingredients and constraints into practical meal ideas.
